About: It is about the Most Holy Theotokos and Ever-Virgin Mary.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with the Blachernae section of Constantinople.
It is associated with the Church of St. Mary of Blachernae.
It is associated with the fall of Constantinople in 1453.
It is associated with Mount Athos.
It is associated with Tsar Alexis.
It is associated with the Dormition Cathedral.
It is associated with the Third Rome doctrine.
It is associated with the Kremlin.
BuiltIn: It was built in the Blachernae section of Constantinople.
ConsistsOf: It consists of wax combined with the ashes of Christian martyrs.
CreatedAround: It was created around the 7th century.
CreatedFor: It was created for the Church built in honour of the Virgin Mary in the Blachernae section of Constantinople.
CreatedIn: It was created in the 7th century.
HasA: It has a sumptuous chasuble glittering with gold and precious stones.
It has similarities to a set of 13th-century icons of St. George.
It has an original Greek inscription.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by being unusual among Orthodox icons in that it is not flat.
It is characterized by its bas relief form.
It is characterized by its composition of wax combined with the ashes of Christian martyrs.
It is characterized by its Orans type.
It is characterized by its Hodegetria style.
It is characterized by its carved high relief.
It is characterized by an original Greek inscription.
HeldIn: It was held in Moscow's main church, the Dormition Cathedral.
It was held in the Kremlin.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by the Third Rome doctrine.
IsA: It is a holy object of Byzantine tradition.
KnownAs: It is known as Theotokos of Blachernae.
It is known as Our Lady of Blachernae.
KnownFor: It is known for being the intercessory protectress of Constantinople and the Eastern Roman Empire.
It is known for its unusual bas relief form.
It is known for its composition of wax and ashes of Christian martyrs.
It is known for its reappearance on Mount Athos.
It is known for its veneration in Muscovy.
LocatedIn: It is located in the Dormition Cathedral.
It is located in the Kremlin.
OccurredDuring: It occurred during the Great Fire of 1812.
OccurredAround: It occurred around the mid-17th century.
OriginatedIn: It originated in the Blachernae quarter.
It originated in the Church of St. Mary.
PartOf: It is part of the Eastern Roman Empire.
It is part of Byzantine tradition.
It is part of the Dormition Cathedral.
It is part of the Kremlin.
UsedAs: It is used as a Byzantine palladium.
It is used as a gift to Tsar Alexis.
It is used as a relic.
UsedFor: It is used for representing the Most Holy Theotokos and Ever-Virgin Mary.
It is used for veneration in Muscovy.
UsedIn: It is used in the Church of St. Mary of Blachernae.
It is used in Moscow's main church, the Dormition Cathedral.
It is used in the Kremlin.
UsedTo: It is used to penetrate the viewer with awe.
